Yeah those guys are reliable. Let us hope we will catch these banditfuckers with their pants down. The plan is easy; we approach – throw our grenades and assault. Are you ready?
Yes I’m ready.
Grenades? I don’t have any…
Shit mate, who comes to an assault without grenades? Here take this one – pay me a drink when we survived this bullshit. \n\nWell, enough chatting, we have to earn our pay now. Ready?
Let’s go!
What a fight! We made it!
Yeah mate, that was crazy! I'm glad to see you made it!
Yeah man, I'm glad to see you to! What will you do?
I'll stay here and guard the passage.
Hey mate, I wanted to thank you for the grenade you gave me.
No problem, I'm sure the bandits would disagree.
[Zone Vodka] Here, this is for you.
[Nemiroff] Here, this is for you.
[Putinka] Here, this is for you.
[Stolichnaya] Here, this is for you.
Wait, I'll get a drink to thank you!
That's mighty cool of you to remember, I will remember this.
No problem mate.
---- What a Night ----
Hey man, how you're doing?
Horrible, my head is splitting itself.
Hey man, how you're doing?
I'm doing formidable my friend, do you want a shot?
You seem to be in high spirit, what happened?
God smiled upon me my friend, my financial worries are no more!
Congrats!
Here have a drink with me!
Sure let it flow!
Nah, I'm fine.
Aaaah... \n \n C'mon, between the first and the second, the break is short!
You don't want, as you want.
I can only agree with you!
Thanks, but I don't want to become drunk.
Here have some meat to chase the burn.
Thanks. So tell me, what happened?
You won't believe it! Yesterday I was drinking with the boys and we played some cards. My luck turned bad and I lost all my money to Andrey. So, this morning I set out in order to make my money back - I figured if I managed to hunt some Flesh or Boars, I could sell their parts for some good money. I set off a bit later in the morning, but the sun was heavy, so I decided to take a short rest in the shadow of the old Church: I had a few bites and some sips of Vodka - to fight my headache, you understand? Suddenly I heard some guys talking behind the wall, what a foul language, you can't imagine! Fuck, I thought, tongues like this only grow on these Renegade Gopniks, so I took my feet into my hands and ran away to the north. Luckily they didn't see me, so my skin was safe... might have forgotten my bottle though now that I think of it. Fuck it! I bypassed the cemetery and made my way to the pond - I figured that maybe I might catch some of these pigs watering. Aah, my friend, my intuition was right, there were two pigs taking a rest on the shore. I snuck up and BAM - shot and missed. My hands were probably still shaky from yesterday evening... doesn't matter! The two pigs squeal up, taking off in the direction of the burned farmstead. I lunch myself in pursuit, but I gave those two such a fright that one run straight into the flame anomaly! The pillar engulfed him immediately, roasting him on the spot. I cursed the heavens, impossible to make money of that pile of ashes now I thought. But then I noticed it... the pig probably kicked a Flame while running through the anomaly, and the artifact landed outside, ripe for the taking! Can you imagine it?
Incredible! What luck!
Indeed, indeed! Let's have a drink to that!
Let's drink!
Ah my friend, I quite enjoy drinking with you! Let's have another one, to friendship!
I like drinking with you too! To friendship!
Here my friend, have a bite.
Alright, my turn to propose a toast!
Let it hear!
Our will shall not break, we drank, drink and will drink again!
To you, to us, to xcvb!
God is not stupid, he loves fives!
Dude, what the fuck, what happened last night? I woke up in Khuyevo Kukuyevo!
Yeah man, you were fucking blasted, hahaha! We caroused quite hard, hahaha. I went back to the camp to get some more booze, but on my return you were gone.
So what happened yesterday?
Ah, that my friend only the Author knows. You'll have to wait until he remembers...
...
Librarian: Oi mate, there you are. Come here for a second.
Forgot about me and your debt? I see how it is asshole, I will remember.
You wanted to talk?
Yes mate, indeed I wanted to talk. I see you're happy to have found your belongings untouched after a night of carousing, yes? \n \nWell, your stuff didn't protect itself, I had to watch it the whole night so that noone gets too curious. I believe my care should be rewarded, don't you mate?
[500 roubles] Sure sure, thank you very much.
You're kidding me right? You got all your stuff back and this is all you give me?
[2.000 roubles] Sure sure, thank you very much.
Hmm, you have trouble parting from your money don't you? Well can't blame you, although I would imagine that your stuff is worth much more to you.
[5.000 roubles] Sure sure, thank you very much.
My man, no trouble at all! I see you're an upstanding lad, here, have a drink to counter this evil headache that must plague you.
I'm sorry mate, I don't have much in my pockets... I'll pay you when I have made some money...
Eh, you have money to carouse, but no money to pay me for savekeeping your belongings? \n \nTell you what mate, pay me tomorrow and I will consider you as a man of honour.
What do you mean? That is more than enough for sitting behind your bar and turning thumbs.
[2.000 roubles] You're right, sorry.
Fuck off then.
Alright mate, no trouble, you're not the first Stalker whose arms are not long enough to reach far into his pockets.
I wanted to pay my debt to you.
Sure mate.
[500 roubles] Here, and thank you very much.
You're kidding me right? You got all your stuff back and this is all you give me?
[2.000 roubles] Here, and thank you very much.
Hmm, you have trouble parting from your money don't you? Well can't blame you, although I would imagine that your stuff is worth much more to you.
[5.000 roubles] Here, and thank you very much.
My man, no trouble at all! I see you're an upstanding lad, here, have a drink to counter this evil headache that must plague you.
I'm sorry mate, I don't have much in my pockets... I'll pay you when I have made some money...
I'm not waiting much longer mate, get to it.
What do you mean? That is more than enough for sitting behind your bar and turning thumbs.
[2.000] You're right, sorry.
Fuck off then.
Alright mate, no trouble, you're not the first Stalker to be a bit stingy with his money.
Here is your CD.
My man!
---- Rush to the North and Brainscorcher reactions ------
Hey Fanatic, how are you doing?
To be honest, I'm overwhelmed with all these new rookies. Ever since you disabled the Brain Scorcher, more and more people are flocking to the Zone. That's not meant as a reproach, you did well to turn that off. Sidorovich is of course esctatic about all the new business opportunities and cheap labour, but I still have to try and train them. There only are so many of them, and they rapidly loose patience and don't want to wait until I have time for them.
I can help if you want to?
You can? That would be great! You know the drill, right? Go shoot some boars, and then show them how to find artifacts! And, ehm, would it trouble you to provide the supplies for them too? It's not a lot, some 20 buckshots, an Echo detector, a Empty Container and a bottle Nemiroff?
Sure Fanatic, the same you provided me.
Thank you very much! Your apprentice will be Iliaz, he's quartering in the brick house on the other side of the street.
What? I'm not a charity.
Ehm, okay... well don't bother then.
Damn, good luck with that.
Well, thanks...
Hey Ilyaz, Fanatic send me to show you some basic skills to survive here.
That's very kind of you Stalker! Where do we start?
First some hunting would be in order. There are some boars east of here, we'll hunt them together.
Alright, I'll be right behind you.
Wait here, I still need to make some preparations.
No problem, I'll wait here.
You see, the boars charge you and you have to dodge them and get some shots in.
I see. What's next?
Next we'll go artifact hunting. There is an anomaly not far from here, a bit north west from the village.
Alright, I'm behind you.
We arrived. Here is a detector which shows you the presence of an artifact. The faster the beeping, the closer you are.
Okay.
You have bolts right? You throw them to see if there is an anomaly in front of you.
Yeah, I have some.
Here is some Vodka. Anomalies are often radiated, or even worse. When you're back to my position, you finish this bottle. It helps.
Understood.
Finally, here is a container. If you find an artifact, you'll put it inside this. This will nullify the radiation of the artifact.
Alright.
Now go and try your luck.
Ay ay Stalker.
Why didn't you go all the way in?
I saw this glimmering under the metal structure over there! Isn't that an Artifact?
What? That's a Stoneflower! You lucky bastard! You can either sell it to Sidorovich for some fast cash, or hang onto it and sell it to the Ecologists, they will pay much better.
Understood! Thanks for the tip!
[Lie] Ah, no luck. That artifact is worthless. But as you're new, I'll take it off your hands for 1000 roubles.
Alright... I guess that's better than nothing.
We should go back to the village now.
Alright, I'll follow you.
We are back at the village. I hope I helped you a bit!
Very much so! Thank you!
---- Shlitzer
And Stalker, are you happy with your pay?
Did the man in the bunker scam you Stalker?
He send me on an assault for only 2.000 roubles.
No, he payed me well.
Ha! Of course he did. Sidorovich taking advantage of rookies like you, a story as old as the Zone itself. But you know, not everyone here pays that miserably. I might have a proposition for you if you're interested.
That would be the first time he would pay anyone well. Maybe you don't know the worth of your labour yet... If you want to find out, I might have a proposition for you.
I'm always interested in some work.
I don't know.
Good, good. But let's not talk here. Follow me.
If you come to a decision, find me in the there to my left.
...
What kind of work do you propose?
That of the sensitive kind - discretion will be necessary and no moral qualms. But I also pay accordingly. Can you be discrete?
Depends. What is it you want me to do?
You need to pick up a client from the Southern Checkpoint - a rich tourist looking for some excitement here. You are to pick him up and escort him to the Northern Farm. Some people are waiting there, and when the client is finished, you're to bring him back to the Southern Checkpoint. Sounds easy right? You'll be payed good money to have a stroll with him - and of course to keep your mouth shut. Understood?
When would the pickup be?
During the night, around 22h to midnight. I already provided him a PDA, here is the contact. You're to wait for him at the jeep wreckage on the road to the Checkpoint. The soldiers are paid not to shoot him - that doesn't apply to you.
What will he be doing in the Northern Farm?
That doesn't concern you, and you're getting paid to not ask such questions. You're the escort, the Zone taxi. You pick him up and drop him off. Easy enough.
I have to think about it.
Alright, but don't take too much time, or I will find someone else to do the job.
Alright, I'll do it.
Great! Remember, after 22h at the jeep wreckage! And don't let the guy die!

Holster your weapon or we will shoot!

Aye. These bastards killed one of my men, and we tracked them down to this part of the Zone. But instead of confronting us, these cowards are hiding themselves in the underground and refuse to come out. Now they are like rats in a trap. The problem is, their position is easily defendable and it will be difficult to smoke them out. At the moment the plan is to starve them out, but I cannot lose too much time here babysitting the exits...
If the pay is good enough, I can do it for you.
If you think you're able to do it! I'm willing to pay good money for them rotting in hell.

Wait, you are not a member of their group, are you?

No I am not, but they send me to take care of you.
You alone? You're either very confident or foolish...
Why did you kill one of them?
The guy took a loan from me, but didn't pay up - so he had to pay with his life. Logical no? I had no choice, or I would have lost all respect.
You said you were ready to parley?
Yes we are. This situation is a stalemate you see - they cannot come in without heavy losses and an uncertain victory, and we cannot get out without suffering the same fate. I am not ready to die in this place, but I believe that our pursuers might not be ready to die either - so I propose that they look the other direction and we slip away. Or else we both are stuck here for a long and lost time.

Convince him! If you do, I'll be sure to reward your skills to do so... If that doesn't work, I might have a second proposition for you. But first we'll try it the diplomatic route.
About your situation...

--- Fanatic
What are the reason most people come here?
There is a silent agreement not to talk about the reasons why other Stalkers are here - most of the time you wouldn't like the answer. Most people come here for the money, out of greed but more often out of necessity, hoping to make a fortune quickly. Stalkers in order to find Artifacts and sell them, or working for the authorities for an increased payroll. \n \nThen there are the one's hiding here from the law - you will find a lot of bastards who slaughtered or worse, and now hide here. And don't make the mistake to think to find these kind only among bandits. \n \nVery few come for an adventure - mostly fools in my opinion. Most of them change their mind when it's too late. \n \nIn the end, it's only misery driving them here, and no one likes to talk about it.
Why are you here?
Sometimes I wonder too...

How did you know that they were bandits?
At first I didn't, but I started to suspect it during the conversation. You see, Stepan was very excited about... the events, so he got eagerly into a conversation with them and quite quickly it turned to... murdering people... and they talked so casualy about it that I...

These guys fucked me! I've met their boss, this Shlitzer guy, when I was in a difficult situation! I needed some money in order to bail out my friend who was held hostage by bandits, money which I didn't have! So I found a guy willing to lend it to me, Shlitzer, cursed be his name... when I went back to the bandits, they told me they found someone else who would pay more money for my friend... What did they do to him? Sold him into slavery? Fuck, I don't know... You wouldn't have met a Stalker named Yulianov Klad, would you? \n \nAh fuck, it doesnt matter, I'm also in deep shit now... Well, I tried to give Shlitzer his money back, but he wanted interests! And what interests! I did every shitjob you can find here in order to pay off my debt, I even risked my life in this fucking arena! But my debt grew faster than I could work. Fuck my life... \n \nSo the day came when I wasn't able to pay one of the due dates... well here I am! But the fucking didn't stop, no! These fuckers "bill" me the food I eat, the water I drink! I will never be free again!
Shit... sounds like these guys fucked you!
Sucks to be you... now get back to work.

No wait Stalker! I beg you, you alone can help me! Please, let me go! Don't you see, I'm a slave, condemned to die being a slave!\n \nStalker, I implore you! Look the other way! Or pretend to be shooting at me! They will be angry, yes, but they won't harm you! This, here, it will be my end if you don't take pitty on me!
Ah fuck it... run, when I'm looking the other way, but be quick about it! I'll fire some shots in the air to make them think I tried to stop you.
What the fuck? Fuck you, and go back to work!
